title: Odyssey, Book 23: Penelope's Test
description: Book 23 of Homer's Odyssey: Penelope's Test.
introduction: Book 23 of the Odyssey: Penelope's Test. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.text: The old nurse ran upstairs laughing to tell Penelope. But Penelope was cautious — what if this was a trick of the gods? She tested Odysseus by ordering their bed moved. He protested: the bed could not be moved, for he had built it himself around a living olive tree. By this secret only they shared, she knew him. They wept and held each other, and Athena held back the dawn.
